failure_mode: No REST API exists - R Shiny app only
root_cause: RepoDB is an R Shiny web application hosted at unmtid-shinyapps.net/shiny/repodb/. The repodb.net website is a static Jekyll site with no API endpoints (returns 404 on /api/ and /download/). The api.repodb.net subdomain has an SSL certificate mismatch. The only programmatic data access is through downloading the SQLite database file, which requires local processing. No REST/JSON API exists.
workaround: Use existing tools: Open Targets (drug-disease associations with clinical trial phases), ChEMBL (drug indications with max_phase_for_ind), ClinicalTrials.gov (16 tools for trial searches), DGIdb (drug-gene interactions). These databases collectively cover the drug repurposing use case with real-time API access.
workaround_tool_file: Already covered by opentarget_tools.json, chembl_tools.json, clinicaltrials_gov_tools.json, dgidb_tools.json
investigation_notes: api.repodb.net has SSL certificate mismatch (cert not valid for api.repodb.net)
unmtid-shinyapps.net/shiny/repodb/ returns 200 but is an R Shiny app (HTML with Shiny JavaScript dependencies)
Data originally from ClinicalTrials.gov drug-indication pairs
RepoDB paper (Brown & Patel, 2017) describes a static database, not a REST service
